assignments.text: mask = riscv::MIP_SSIP | riscv::MIP_STIP | riscv::MIP_SEIP | riscv::MIP_MSIP | riscv::MIP_MTIP;

always_blocks.header: always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin
always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin
always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin

reset_facts.text_preview:
input  logic                         clk_i,
  input  logic                         rst_ni,
  // Core ID, Cluster ID and boot address are considered more or less static
  input  logic [63:0]                  boot_addr_i,  // reset boot address
  input  logic [63:0]                  hart_id_i,    // hart id in a multicore environment (reflected in a CSR)

.clk_i                  ( clk_i                       ),
    .rst_ni                 ( rst_ni                      ),
    .flush_i                ( flush_ctrl_ex               ),
    .fu_data_i              ( fu_data_id_ex               ),
    .pc_i                   ( pc_id_ex                    ),
    .is_compressed_instr_i  ( is_compressed_instr_id_ex   ),

.clk_i             ( clk_i                  ),
    .rst_ni            ( rst_ni                 ),
    .debug_mode_i      ( debug_mode             ),
    .addr_i            ( addr_csr_perf          ),
    .we_i              ( we_csr_perf            ),
    .data_i            ( data_csr_perf          ),

.clk_i                 ( clk_i                       ),
    .rst_ni                ( rst_ni                      ),
    // I$
    .icache_en_i           ( icache_en_csr               ),
    .icache_flush_i        ( icache_flush_ctrl_cache     ),
    .icache_miss_o         ( icache_miss_cache_perf      ),

.clk_i                 ( clk_i                       ),
    .rst_ni                ( rst_ni                      ),
    .priv_lvl_i            ( priv_lvl                    ),
    // I$
    .icache_en_i           ( icache_en_csr               ),
    .icache_flush_i        ( icache_flush_ctrl_cache     ),

reset_facts.signal_or_condition: always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin
always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin

reset_facts.text_preview:
input  logic                  clk_i,                      // Clock
    input  logic                  rst_ni,                     // Asynchronous reset active low
    input  logic                  time_irq_i,                 // Timer threw a interrupt
    // send a flush request out if a CSR with a side effect has changed (e.g. written)
    output logic                  flush_o,
    output logic                  halt_csr_o,                 // halt requested

reset_facts.signal_or_condition: always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin

csr_facts.text: function automatic logic [31:0] csrw (csr_reg_t csr, logic[4:0] rs1);
function automatic logic [31:0] csrr (csr_reg_t csr, logic [4:0] dest);
function string spikeCommitLog(logic [63:0] pc, priv_lvl_t priv_lvl, logic [31:0] instr, logic [4:0] rd, logic [63:0] result, logic rd_fpr);
return $sformatf("%d 0x%h (0x%h) %s 0x%h\n", priv_lvl, pc, instr, rd_s, result);
$fwrite(f, "%d 0x%0h %s (0x%h) DASM(%h)\n", cycles, commit_instr_id_commit[i].pc, mode, commit_instr_id_commit[i].ex.tval[31:0], commit_instr_id_commit[i].ex.tval[31:0]);
$fwrite(f, "Exception Cause: Illegal Instructions, DASM(%h) PC=%h\n", commit_instr_id_commit[i].ex.tval[31:0], commit_instr_id_commit[i].pc);
$fwrite(f, "%d 0x%0h %s (0x%h) DASM(%h)\n", cycles, commit_instr_id_commit[i].pc, mode, commit_instr_id_commit[i].ex.tval[31:0], commit_instr_id_commit[i].ex.tval[31:0]);
$fwrite(f, "Exception Cause: %5d, DASM(%h) PC=%h\n", commit_instr_id_commit[i].ex.cause, commit_instr_id_commit[i].ex.tval[31:0], commit_instr_id_commit[i].pc);
end else if (!debug_mode_q && csr_op_i == WFI && !ex_i.valid) begin

asset_candidates.name: mstatus
asset_candidates.description: Machine-mode status register controlling global interrupt enable and privilege stack; holds fields such as MIE, MPIE, MPP, etc.

asset_candidates.name: mip
asset_candidates.description: Machine interrupt pending register; indicates pending interrupts.

asset_candidates.name: mie
asset_candidates.description: Machine interrupt enable register; controls which interrupts are enabled.

asset_candidates.name: mepc
asset_candidates.description: Machine exception program counter; holds return address on exception.

asset_candidates.name: mcause
asset_candidates.description: Machine cause register; records the reason for an exception or interrupt.

asset_candidates.name: mtval
asset_candidates.description: Machine trap value register; stores faulting address or instruction information.

asset_candidates.name: satp
asset_candidates.description: Supervisor address translation and protection register; controls virtual memory and holds root page-table pointer and ASID.

asset_candidates.name: pmpcfg0
asset_candidates.description: Physical memory protection configuration register; defines permission and matching for physical addresses.
asset_candidates.note: PMP registers may be present; not all signals listed, but static facts show CSR_PMPCFG0 = 12'h3A0

asset_candidates.name: pmpaddr0
asset_candidates.description: Physical memory protection address register; holds address bounds for PMP check.

asset_candidates.name: sstatus
asset_candidates.description: Supervisor-mode status register; contains fields like SPP, SPIE, SIE, etc.

asset_candidates.name: sepc
asset_candidates.description: Supervisor exception program counter.

asset_candidates.name: scause
asset_candidates.description: Supervisor cause register.

asset_candidates.name: stval
asset_candidates.description: Supervisor trap value register.

asset_candidates.name: dcsr
asset_candidates.description: Debug control and status register; controls debug mode operation and step/halt behavior.

asset_candidates.name: dpc
asset_candidates.description: Debug program counter; holds the PC when entering debug mode.

asset_candidates.name: dscratch0
asset_candidates.description: Debug scratch register 0, general-purpose in debug mode.

asset_candidates.name: dscratch1
asset_candidates.description: Debug scratch register 1.

asset_candidates.name: mideleg/medeleg
asset_candidates.description: Machine trap delegation registers; control which traps are delegated to supervisor mode.

asset_candidates.name: mtvec/stvec
asset_candidates.description: Trap-vector base addresses for machine and supervisor mode.

asset_candidates.name: mcounteren/scounteren
asset_candidates.description: Counter-enable registers governing access to performance counters from lower privilege modes.

asset_candidates.name: performance counters
asset_candidates.description: Read-only counters (cycle, instret, cache misses, etc.) with read access controlled by counter enable.

asset_candidates.name: debug_mode state
asset_candidates.description: The debug_mode_q signal indicating whether the hart is in debug mode; once set, it grants special access to debug CSRs and possibly other resources.

asset_candidates.name: tvm, tw, tsr fields
asset_candidates.description: Mstatus fields that trap supervisor virtual-memory accesses, WFI, and SRET instructions, respectively; act as security controls.

subject_candidates.name: machine-mode CPU
subject_candidates.description: CPU executing in machine mode (PRIV_LVL_M). Has highest privilege for CSR access and configuration.
related_signals: umode_i (inverted logic, actual priv level derived from mstatus)
subject_candidates.note: Privilege level likely derived from mstatus.mpp or internal state; static facts define PRIV_LVL_M=2'b11.

subject_candidates.name: supervisor-mode CPU
subject_candidates.description: CPU in supervisor mode (PRIV_LVL_S). Has access to a subset of CSRs, subject to delegation and counter-enable.

subject_candidates.name: user-mode CPU
subject_candidates.description: CPU in user mode (PRIV_LVL_U). Most privileged CSRs are inaccessible.

subject_candidates.name: external debug interface
subject_candidates.description: Debug request input (debug_req_i) that can force the hart into debug mode, potentially bypassing normal execution and accessing debug CSRs and internal state.

subject_candidates.name: commit stage
subject_candidates.description: The commit stage issues CSR write/read operations based on committed instructions. It provides the CSR address, write data, and operands.

subject_candidates.name: pipeline control
subject_candidates.description: Controller and other pipeline stages that can generate flushes, fences, and influence CSR state (e.g., fence_i, sfence_vma).

operation_candidates.name: CSR read
operation_candidates.description: Reading the value of a CSR, initiated by a CSR instruction (CSRRS, CSRRC, etc.) and returning csr_rdata_o.

operation_candidates.name: CSR write
operation_candidates.description: Writing a value to a CSR, initiated by CSR instructions or possibly debug mode. May use read-modify-write operations.

operation_candidates.name: clear/set bits
operation_candidates.description: Bit-wise clear (CSRRC/CSRRCI) or set (CSRRS/CSRRSI) operations on CSRs.
related_signals: fu_op encoding indicating CSRRC, CSRRS, etc.

operation_candidates.name: debug mode entry
operation_candidates.description: Entering debug mode via debug_req_i assertion; halts normal execution and allows debug operations.

operation_candidates.name: debug mode exit
operation_candidates.description: Exiting debug mode via dret instruction, clearing debug_mode_q and resuming normal execution.

operation_candidates.name: flush and fence
operation_candidates.description: Pipeline flushes and fence operations (fence.i, sfence.vma) that affect caches, TLBs, and can indirectly affect security state.

operation_candidates.name: performance counter access
operation_candidates.description: Reading performance counters, controlled by mcounteren/scounteren and privilege level.

guard_candidates.name: privilege level check
guard_candidates.description: CSR access is permitted only if the current privilege level is at least the minimum required by the CSR (machine-only, supervisor+machine, etc.).
guard_candidates.mechanism: comparison of current priv_lvl with CSR address category

guard_candidates.name: counter enable check
guard_candidates.description: Performance counter accesses from lower privilege modes are gated by MCOUNTEREN/SCOUNTEREN bits.

guard_candidates.name: tvm bit
guard_candidates.description: When set in mstatus, traps supervisor virtual-memory management accesses (sfence.vma, satp accesses) to machine mode.

guard_candidates.name: tw bit
guard_candidates.description: When set, WFI instruction in supervisor mode traps to machine mode.

guard_candidates.name: tsr bit
guard_candidates.description: When set, SRET instruction in supervisor mode traps to machine mode.

guard_candidates.name: debug mode authorization
guard_candidates.description: Access to debug CSRs (dcsr, dpc, dscratch) is only allowed when debug_mode_q is true.

guard_candidates.name: amo_valid_commit filter for debug request
guard_candidates.description: The internal debug_req signal is gated to prevent debug entry while an atomic memory operation is committing, potentially avoiding inconsistent state.

guard_candidates.name: PMP check
guard_candidates.description: Physical memory protection units (PMPCFG/PMPADDR) enforce access rules on physical addresses for load/store and possibly CSR-based accesses.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: debug_mode_q
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Debug mode state. Set when debug_req_i is asserted and amo_valid_commit is low. Cleared on dret. Holds the hart in halted state for debug operations.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: mtvec_rst_load_q
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Indicates that mtvec has been loaded at reset, likely to redirect PC to boot address after reset until first mtvec write.
state_lifecycle_facts.set_condition: After reset until mtvec is written? Exact logic not in static facts but signal name implies a one-shot reset load state.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: mip clearing
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Machine interrupt pending bits are cleared by writing to specific bits in mip (via CSR writes). Bits set by external interrupt lines (irq_i, ipi_i, time_irq_i).

state_lifecycle_facts.name: cycle_q, instret_q
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Performance counters increment each cycle/retired instruction. Read via performance counter mechanism. Reset to 0?

state_lifecycle_facts.name: dirty_fp_state_csr
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Flag indicating floating-point state is dirty; used to manage lazy FP context save. Set when FP instruction retires, cleared on FS context clean operation.

path_candidates.name: debug interface to debug CSRs
path_candidates.description: External debug_req_i sets debug_mode_q. While debug_mode_q is high, the debug module can read/write debug CSRs (dcsr, dpc, dscratch0/1) via a debug bus interface, bypassing normal instruction-based CSR access and potentially privilege checks.
path_candidates.subject: external debug interface

path_candidates.name: debug interface to non-debug CSRs
path_candidates.description: Potential path from debug mode to access arbitrary CSRs, including those normally restricted to machine mode. If the debug module implementation allows CSR read/write while in debug mode without re-checking privilege, debug could read/write mstatus, satp, pmp, etc.
path_candidates.subject: external debug interface
path_candidates.guard: possibly only debug_mode_q, missing normal privilege check

path_candidates.name: debug mode exit escape
path_candidates.description: If dret instruction does not properly restore the previous privilege level or clears mstatus fields incorrectly, debug mode could be used to elevate privileges.
path_candidates.subject: debug interface

path_candidates.name: normal CSR access via instruction
path_candidates.description: CPU pipeline fetches and commits a CSR instruction. Commit stage sends csr_addr_i, fu_op, csr_wdata_i to csr_regfile. The module checks privilege and possibly tvm/tw/tsr before allowing read/write.
path_candidates.subject: machine-mode/supervisor-mode/user-mode CPU
path_candidates.guard: privilege level check, counter enable, tvm/tw/tsr

path_candidates.name: performance counter read by lower privilege
path_candidates.description: Lower privilege (U/S) attempts to read performance counters. Access gated by mcounteren/scounteren bits. If those bits are incorrectly set or the check is missing, lower privilege could observe cycle/instret counts which may be security-sensitive.
path_candidates.subject: user/supervisor mode

path_candidates.name: PMP bypass via debug mode
path_candidates.description: If debug mode operations (e.g., direct memory reads) bypass PMP checks, external debug could access protected memory regions.
path_candidates.subject: debug interface

summary: Identified security-sensitive assets include CSR registers controlling privilege, interrupts, debug, and memory protection. Subjects include CPU privilege levels (machine, supervisor, user) and external debug interface. Operations are CSR read/write, debug mode entry/exit, and performance counter access. Guards include privilege checks, counter-enable, and mstatus fields (tvm, tw, tsr). State lifecycle covers debug mode and reset behavior. Potential vulnerability paths involve debug mode bypassing normal CSR access controls, debug access to non-debug CSRs, and counter exposure.
